channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: Elon Musk loses court battle against Sam Altman and OpenAI after 3-week trial
item.description: A jury in Oakland, California, ruled against Elon Musk in his dramatic court battle with Sam Altman and OpenAI.

item.title: Trump pivots to midterms, affordability after China summit as Iran war persists
item.description: Trump, at a White House event Monday, is expected to announce an expansion of the number of discounted prescription drugs offered through TrumpRX.

item.title: Warren Buffett teased to CNBC a ‘tiny purchase’ in March. Berkshire filing may have revealed it
item.description: When asked in March whether Berkshire was still putting money to work, Buffett said the conglomerate had made "one tiny purchase."

item.title: S&P 500, Nasdaq fall for a second day as Micron drops, traders eye oil and yields: Live updates
item.description: Traders monitored oil prices and bond yields while awaiting further developments with the conflict in the Middle East.

item.title: Seagate leads memory sell-off as CEO says it would 'take too long' to build new factories
item.description: Seagate CEO Dave Mosley's comment sent shares sinking, along with those of Micron, SanDisk and Western Digital.

item.title: Retail stocks are ailing on fears about the U.S. consumer. Will these earnings change that?
item.description: Pivots to consumer staples from discretionary are standard when warning signs start to flash.

item.title: Kevin Warsh to be sworn in as Federal Reserve chair on Friday
item.description: Trump will swear in Kevin Warsh, his hand-picked choice to lead the Federal Reserve, during a White House ceremony.

item.title: U.S. creates $1.7B 'lawfare' fund in exchange for Trump dropping $10B IRS suit
item.description: Trump sued the IRS in late January over the leak of his tax information by an IRS employee Charles "Chaz" Littlejohn in 2019 and 2020.

item.title: The Fed will have to raise interest rates in July to appease 'bond vigilantes,' Yardeni says
item.description: Sent to the Federal Reserve to lower interest rates, incoming Chair Kevin Warsh instead may have to push for higher levels.

item.title: Meta layoffs starting this week stress harsh AI reality inside Zuckerberg’s company
item.description: Meta is starting layoffs this week, with 8,000 jobs expected to be cut, as employees brace for a new era of AI.

item.title: New bipartisan bill would let retirees use their 401(k) to make direct charitable donations
item.description: Under current law, qualified charitable distributions — which are available to people age 70 1/2 — can only be made from individual retirement accounts.

item.title: The last century was great for U.S. stocks, but the next decade might be challenging, says investing chief
item.description: High valuations and narrow concentration could present challenges for the S&P 500 moving forward, one expert says.

item.title: Stocks making the biggest moves midday: Regeneron, Dominion, Mobileye, Viking and more
item.description: These are the stocks posting the largest moves in midday trading.

item.title: Nvidia earnings call drama: Will Jensen Huang talk 'Trump' and China chips after Xi summit?
item.description: Both Nvidia's earnings and its commentary on its financial report are expected to be big tests for U.S. equities.

item.title: Trump ballroom money in question after Senate parliamentarian rules. Thune says GOP will persist
item.description: Senate Majority Leader John Thune, R-S.D., said the GOP will refine its approach to seeking money for President Donald Trump's White House ballroom.

item.title: Ford lands its first customer for energy business. Buy this pivot by the auto giant?
item.description: Shares have proven volatile after the carmaker unveiled its new Ford Energy unit, but investors should still watch the name, several analysts said.

item.title: Judge blocks evidence from Luigi Mangione backpack in UnitedHealthcare CEO murder case
item.description: Luigi Mangione was apprehended by police in a McDonald's in Altoona, Pennsylvania, days after the killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson.

item.title: Higher aluminum prices are powering one former Dow component. Here's how to buy for less
item.description: Despite elevated aluminum production costs and shipment timing issues, Alcoa (AA) is strongly capitalizing on structurally higher prices.

item.title: Trump says he should've asked for 'more' of Intel when negotiating stake with CEO
item.description: The chipmaker's stock has soared since the U.S. equity deal in August gave the government 9.9% of the company.

item.title: Lululemon takes battle with Chip Wilson public, calls founder 'misguided' and 'outdated'
item.description: Lululemon responded publicly to founder Chip Wilson's campaign in a letter to shareholders, where it urged them to vote in its favor at its annual meeting.

item.title: It's not too late to buy Nvidia. Here are the reasons we say 'own it, don't trade it'
item.description: "If you wanted to buy some here, I totally endorse it. I just feel that we own it, we don't wanna trade it," Jim Cramer said Monday.

item.title: How to plan a trip for medical tourism and the best resources
item.description: If you're seeking a medical procedure abroad, these resources can help.

item.title: Berkshire Hathaway has revamped its portfolio — Here's how the new stocks are trading
item.description: Berkshire Hathaway took a $2.6 billion stake in Delta Airlines and increased its shares in Alphabet by 224%.

item.title: Homebuilder sentiment improves on late spring surge in demand
item.description: Builders are feeling slightly better about the housing market, as they see improved buyer traffic in a potential late spring surge.

item.title: ‘This is bad’: Strategists see European oil shortages within weeks as inventories are depleted
item.description: The Iran war oil squeeze could trigger shortages in Europe within weeks, and keep global inventories depleted until 2027, strategists warn.

item.title: Ominous bond trades point to much higher rates
item.description: The put bets would be a bet on a yield spike since bond prices (value of the TLT fund) move inversely to rates.

item.title: Ryanair has plans for 'armageddon' scenario as CFO warns weaker European carriers may not survive jet fuel crunch
item.description: Ryanair said it has plans for an "armageddon situation," amid the jet fuel crisis.

item.title: White House touts deals on soybeans and rare earths after Trump-Xi summit, while China talks up tariff cuts
item.description: The meeting between U.S. President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping last week yielded new pacts, though the sides have provided differing details.

item.title: Bank of England does not need to hike interest rates, says IMF — it may even need to cut
item.description: The Bank of England is expected to hold and even potentially hike interest rates this year in response to resurgent inflation sparked by the Iran war.

item.title: Why a small UK lender has major U.S. credit firms on edge
item.description: The continuing fallout from MFS' collapse has heightened fears that problems in complex credit markets pose a broader systemic risk.
